arts.ttl: Birmingham City predicted XI vs Stockport as Chris Davies considers three big calls - Joseph Chapman - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Stansfield, Cochrane, Klarer Willumsson, Iwata, Paik Seung-ho and Ryan Allsop are all in line to return for the bread and butter of Blues' season. Jay Stansfield, Alex Cochrane and Willum Willumsson stayed in Birmingham, while Tomoki Iwata, Christoph Klarer and Ben Davies were involved but weren't needed .

arts.ttl: Lyndon Dykes opens up after Scotland tribulations and 'difficult' Birmingham City start
arts.sum: I think there were a lot of fresh legs at Blackpool and I thought it just shows how hard we've been working on the training pitch, to keep fit, because when the time comes and we're needed we have to be ready. "I've scored goals this season when I've got minutes.

arts.ttl: Dykes cashed in to leave Chris Davies with something to muse in Birmingham City refresh - Joseph Chapman - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: All associated with Blues will now be tuning in on Monday night to watch as the draw for the third round is made - and with it enter all clubs from the Championship and the Premier League. Birmingham City eased themselves into the third round of the FA Cup with a professional performance and goals for Lukas Jutkiewicz and Lyndon Dykes at Blackpool on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Wayne Rooney update emerges as 'clock is now ticking' 11 months after Birmingham City exit
arts.sum: The 39-year-old lasted just three months after replacing John Eustace in October 2023 who had City in sixth, with the former England international taking the club down to 20th place in the Championship, winning just two games out of 15. Argyle lost 4-0 away to Bristol City on Saturday, just four days after a humiliating 6-1 defeat at home to Norwich City, leaving Rooney's men languishing in 21st place in the Championship.
